steps.title: Spot the key contradiction
steps.content: The passage pivots with “And yet the old man was secretly proud of his acquisition.” This phrase marks a contradiction: while he openly criticizes the picture (“Don't belong in here,” he fumed), he is internally proud of owning it.

steps.title: Apply to the main purpose
steps.content:
Choice A is correct because it states the main purpose accurately: the passage reveals the old man's conflicting feelings toward the picture.

The other choices miss this dynamic—
  • B focuses on the seller rather than the picture itself
  • C treats the shop inventory as the main subject
  • D merely describes a mismatch without explaining its significance
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
